title: Control Structures: if, for, while
summary: Control program flow with conditional statements and loops.
content_html:
Control structures decide which code runs and how many times.

if / elif / else

if score >= 90:
    grade = 'A'
elif score >= 70:
    grade = 'B'
else:
    grade = 'C'

for Loop

for i in range(5):     # i = 0,1,2,3,4
    print(i)

for item in ['a','b','c']:
    print(item)

while Loop

count = 0
while count < 5:
    print(count)
    count += 1

break and continue

for i in range(10):
    if i == 5: break    # exits loop
    if i % 2 == 0: continue  # skips even numbers
    print(i)

Common Mistakes

  • Infinite loop: forgetting to update the while condition
  • Off-by-one: range(n) gives 0 to n-1, not 1 to n
  • Indentation errors in Python (indentation defines blocks)

exercises.title: if condition
exercises.prompt: In Python, the 'if' block runs when:

exercises.title: while loop condition
options: As long as the condition is True
exercises.answer: As long as the condition is True

exercises.title: continue
options: Skips the rest of the current iteration and continues with the next
exercises.answer: Skips the rest of the current iteration and continues with the next

exercises.title: for vs while
exercises.prompt: When is a 'while' loop preferred over 'for'?
options: When the number of iterations is unknown
When iterating a fixed number of times
exercises.answer: When the number of iterations is unknown
